intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text:
Good! I see three 10x samples here. You've asked for the one prefixed **GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1**. I also notice there are actually two more samples in the data folder (GSM5746269 and GSM5746270), but I'll focus on GSM5746268 as requested.

Now let me read the Seurat skill to base the workflow on the current best practices:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Perfect. The recipe shows Seurat v5 with key decision points: QC thresholds, number of PCs, and clustering resolution. Let me present a plan:

input.title: Seurat v5 workflow for GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1
input.summary: Load 10x triplet, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and find top markers per cluster.

steps.title: Load 10x matrix triplet and create Seurat object
steps.description: Read barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz, matrix.mtx.gz; call ReadMtx to build a dgCMatrix; create Seurat object with min.cells=3, min.features=200.

steps.title: QC: calculate metrics and subset
steps.description: Add percent.mt (mitochondrial %), nFeature_RNA, nCount_RNA. Visualize with violin plots. Subset on QC thresholds — standard: nFeature_RNA 200–5000, percent.mt < 10% (adjust for tissue type if needed).

steps.title: FindNeighbors, FindClusters, RunUMAP
steps.description: Use top PCs (default 30, adjust if elbow suggests otherwise), build kNN graph, cluster at resolution 0.8 (standard), embed with UMAP.

steps.title: FindAllMarkers and report top per cluster
steps.description: Run FindAllMarkers (Wilcoxon test, threshold logfc=0.25, min.pct=0.1). Extract top 5 markers per cluster by avg_log2FC.
expected_outputs: Marker gene table (cluster, gene, avg_log2FC, p_val_adj)
FeaturePlot of top 1–2 markers per cluster

assumptions: Using GSM5746268 sample only (the first triplet).
Mouse genes (MGI0369), so mitochondrial prefix is '^MT-' or '^mt-'.
Standard QC thresholds: nFeature_RNA 200–5000, percent.mt < 10% (will inspect histograms first).
PCA dims: default 30, validated with elbow plot.
Seurat v5 with default layer-based workflow.
